preamble: The shift from a retroflex stop [ʈ] to a glottal stop [ʔ] is a severe form of debuccalization. It typically occurs in syllable-coda positions when a language's phonology simplifies toward an open-syllable structure, or as a strategy to reduce complex articulatory gestures like retroflexion.

examples.note: Historical stops in coda positions have systematically debuccalized into glottal stops.
Widespread historical debuccalization of stop consonants across several Sulawesi languages.
